cot_prefix: Okay, let's take a look at the question and the given text to determine the correct answer. The question is asking what we can infer from Toy Fair 2014, and the options are A, B, C, or D. The text mentions that there's a trend towards education with STEAM (science, technology, engineering, art, and math), including word games and high-tech games. However, it also says that "everything is really big and really out there," suggesting that fun is still a big part of the toys. The text doesn't say toys are becoming more educational than fun, just that there's a push towards education. So option A might not be the correct inference. Option B: "Robots are great favorites of buyers." The text mentions that "we saw lots of products making a push towards education, with STEAM... And of course, we saw some super cool, high-tech games." Also, Adrienne Appell says, "This year we saw lots of 3D games, and robots." However, the text doesn't explicitly state that robots are great favorites of buyers. It only states that they were present and part of the trend. So while robots are mentioned, the text doesn't directly say that they are the favorites. So option B might not be the correct inference. Option C: "Big toys will be popular this year." The text says that "In 2014, everything is really big and really out there," according to Adrienne Appell. It also mentions that "Larger Than Life" was a Toy Fair favorite and that this trend is about big toys with big play value. This suggests that big toys will indeed be popular in 2014. So option C seems like a valid inference. Option D: "Simple card games will disappear on the market."
